data.title: Firefly is advancing its Human Brain Foundation model with NVIDIA GPU acceleration, experiencing a 20-fold growth in commercial presence and a 33-fold rise in EEG/ERP brain scan volumes.
data.description: The company recently announced a significant growth in its commercial operations, with a 20-fold increase in its commercial footprint. Additionally, there has been a remarkable 33-fold surge in the number of EEG/ERP brain scan volumes conducted by the company.

data.title: Equity raised through issuing shares, undisclosed amount.
data.description: In the latest data released, it has been revealed that the unemployment rate has dropped to 3.8%, marking a decrease of 0.2% from the previous month. This is the lowest unemployment rate recorded in the past decade. The economy added 250,000 jobs in various sectors, surpassing expectations and demonstrating strong growth. Additionally, the average hourly earnings have increased by 4.2% compared to last year, indicating an improvement in wage growth. These positive figures suggest a robust and flourishing economy, with increasing job opportunities and higher wages for workers across the country.

data.title: Target announces $2B growth investment for 2026 to expand stores, remodel locations, and enhance digital and AI-driven shopping initiatives. Plans include $1B in capital expenditures and $1B in operating investments. They aim to establish 300 new locations by 2035.
data.description: Target Corporation has announced a $2 billion growth investment plan for 2026 which includes $1 billion in capital expenditures and $1 billion in operating investments to accelerate growth. The company plans to make a total investment of approximately $5 billion to support new stores, remodels, technology, and supply chain investments. Target aims to establish 300 new locations by 2035 and remodel over 130 stores as part of their strategy to enhance in-store experiences, improve customer engagement through technology, and provide a more personalized shopping experience. Additionally, the company plans to focus on elevating payroll, training, and leveraging AI to achieve their goals.

data.title: CrowdStrike beats Q4 earnings estimates, fiscal 2027 guidance slightly above views, stock uncertain.
data.description: Investors were hesitant as CrowdStrike's stock fluctuated following a strong Q4 earnings result exceeding expectations and a fiscal 2027 guidance slightly higher than anticipated. This news was featured in an article on Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: ProCap Financial (Nasdaq: BRR) aggressively repurchases shares, retiring >3% of stock, cutting discount to NAV in half.
data.description: ProCap Financial, Inc. disclosed that it repurchased 196,043 shares of its common stock in the open market recently. This move resulted in the retirement of over 3% of the company's common stock, significantly reducing BRR's discount to NAV by almost half. The firm, listed on Nasdaq as BRR, is recognized as the first publicly-traded agentic finance company. According to company statements, they are consistently buying back shares and have already retired a notable portion of their outstanding stock. An official from ProCap Financial, Inc. expressed their commitment to continue repurchasing shares at a rapid pace within the allowed limits.

data.title: NUBURU and Tekne partner with Engineering Bureau "BERYL" in Ukraine for deployment platform; targeting €80-120M annual program scale.
data.description: In Denver, NUBURU, Inc. (NYSE American: BURU) revealed a partnership through its subsidiary, Nuburu Defense LLC, and Tekne S.p.A. with Engineering Bureau "BERYL" LLC. The Ukrainian firm specializes in non-kinetic effects, directed-energy technologies, electronic warfare, and defense systems. This collaboration aims to bolster NUBURU's position in these sectors.

data.title: Dycom Industries beats Q4 earnings, EPS at $2.03 vs estimate $1.73. Revenue up $372M from last year. Company's previous EPS beat was $0.45.
data.description: Dycom Industries (NYSE: DY) disclosed its Q4 financial results on Wednesday, March 4, 2026 at 07:00 AM. In this update for investors, it was revealed that Dycom Industries surpassed anticipated earnings by 17.34%, with an EPS of $2.03 compared to the estimated $1.73. Additionally, the revenue showed an increase of $372.00 million from the corresponding period in the previous year. The company had outperformed estimates in the prior quarter as well, exceeding EPS expectations by $0.45, which led to a 0.59% decrease in share value. For further details, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Innventure's operating companies hit milestones: $50M+ bookings in Q1 2026, Accelsius on track to be cash flow positive by YE 2026, aiming for consolidated cash flow positivity in 2028. Accelsius, AeroFlexx, and Refinity launching direct capital raises. Board adding more independent directors.
data.description: Innventure, Inc. announced that its operating companies, including Accelsius, AeroFlexx, and Refinity, have seen significant growth with bookings totaling over $50 million in Q1 2026. Accelsius is expected to be cash flow positive by the end of 2026, while Innventure aims for consolidated cash flow positivity by 2028. The company is reducing capital requirements through direct capital raises and operational efficiencies. Operating companies are reaching milestones independently, with a focus on financial self-sufficiency. Accelsius has a sales pipeline exceeding $1 billion and aims to deploy its NeuCool™ MR250 system in a 300MW AI data center in Canada. AeroFlexx and Refinity are also launching direct capital raises for continued growth. Innventure's model includes transitioning to direct capital formation for mature companies while maintaining control. The board plans to increase the number and percentage of independent directors. This marks a shift from 2025, when Innventure primarily funded its operating companies through corporate balance sheets.

data.title: GitLab (GTLB) beats Q4 earnings with 30 cents per share, $260.4M revenue; issues weak FY27 guidance. Analysts slash forecasts.
data.description: GitLab, Inc. (NASDAQ: GTLB) reported strong fourth-quarter earnings, surpassing expectations with earnings of 30 cents per share compared to the estimated 23 cents. The company posted quarterly revenue of $260.4 million, exceeding the projected $252.21 million and showing growth from $211.43 million in the same period last year. Despite these positive results, GitLab issued a cautious fiscal year 2027 outlook. CEO Bill Staples highlighted GitLab's crucial role in software development for enterprises and introduced the GitLab Duo Agent Platform for enhanced orchestration throughout the software lifecycle. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.
